title: CAP25 v13 Route-D shared barrier map
what_this_is: A provenance-gated map of the integrated Route-D state at the two deployed rows (KoalaBear MCA a+=1116048, Mersenne-31 list a+=1116023) - no closure claimed, no new bounds, no row-sharp Q claim.
nonclaims: does not close prob:row-sharp-q or def:q-row-atom
proves no deployed safe row (no U(a+)<=B* certificate)
derives no new mathematics; every node and edge is a citation
reflects the tree at 84b393e and decays as new work lands

lineages.scottdhughes: KB-MCA Route-D residual free-1 / A_SP card (curated by the maintainer from PR #423)
lineages.DannyExperiments: M31 moment-order and Chebyshev fixed-remainder floors (PRs #424/#426)
lineages.holmbuar: row-sharp Q Fourier lineage (#407 kappa, #412 p^{w/2} floor, #414/#416 participation ratio, #417 lift-class refutation, KB/M31 rung audits)
lineages.AllenGrahamHart: independent row-sharp Q calibration (#405); cited as consistency context in #407

nodes.statement: KB-MCA Route-D deployed row: n=2^21, e=w+1=67472, core m_c=913632, free_core=846161, complement n'=A+e=1183520, pack ceiling floor(n'/e)=17, p=2130706433.
Free-1 high families F_H are pairwise disjoint; |F_H|<=floor(n/e)=31; e*p=t*p=143763024447376 is the pair budget. Cross-high (iota,delta) collision remains OPEN.
Residual R2 / H_R2 after SR+H_M; card closes if |R2|<=e*p or |H_R2|<=H2. H2=77291948627, e*p=143763024447376.
R2 = R2_unt xor R2_D; packing |R2|<=930*|H_R2|; the envelope |H_R2|<=n is REFUTED on A_SP residual (max H_R2=952 toy).

nodes.status: MIXED(e=2 CLOSED; unrestricted star e>=3 REFUTED)
nodes.statement: |H|<=p^{e-1}; e=2 => |H|<=p<=H2 CLOSED; unrestricted star for e>=3 is REFUTED on cyclic domains (p^2>H2). Deployed e=67472.
Co-extension free-1 = prefix free-1 multipads; |H_coext(C)|<=H_*^pre(min(C),e); multipad forces t in [2e,n']=[134944,1183520], floor(n'/e)=17.
U2e PROVED (char!=2): at most one free-1 bipartition of a 2e-set; hence H_*^pre(t,e)<=C(t,2e) unconditional. t<=2e+2 => <=H2 (C(2e+2,2)=9105143985<=H2 but C(2e+3,3)=409570621781265>H2).
C_unique PROVED: every untyped free-1 CS pair has the terminal core C_*={n',...,n-1}; N_C=1; hence |H_unt|<=H_*^pre(n',e). Deployed n'=1183520.
nodes.evidence_quote: Every untyped free-1 CS pair has core

nodes.statement: Pure-untyped highs are terminal stars U_* containing index n'-1; |H_unt|=|T|<=C(n'-1,e-1); pack-k alone refuted as an H2 strategy; deployed n'-2e=1048576=2^20 so both binomials >> H2.
THE STANDING WALL: for e>2 the residual free-1 card needs |T|<=H2 (free-1 partner count for e-sets through terminal index n'-1); e=2 CLOSED; alternate open close is |R2|<=e*p. Deployed e=67472.

If the four branch realizations hold and |R_D|<=t, the top-seam compiler concludes unpaid supports <= t*p = 143763024447376; against target_floor=274836936291722953 the paid+retained bound (t*p+11440) leaves integer slack 274693173267264137 (~57.9 bits, ~10.9 multiplicative bits). Deployed agreement a+=1116048, K_rem=4805007.

nodes.status: CONDITIONAL obligation(Rule 1 descent PROVED; cost ledger OPEN)
nodes.statement: Rule 1 is proved as an exact planted-switch core-fiber descent, but its printed support cost |G_{beta,A}|-1 must be accepted by a planted/core ledger; the deletion is not zero-cost.

Rule 2 is realized as a marked multiplicity-aware SP/Pade certificate with exact support cost one; the full-rank primitive residual reduces to fixed-key split-shift sets X_{r,c,U0,H,beta}(z), still needing a printed finite bound N_WSP_full(z)<=B_WSP_full.
A child separated by distance d(S,S')>=r+1 is paid only if a strict-distance Route-D/RIM/window-shadow payment theorem is imported; otherwise it is a new payment obligation.
All charged top-seam rows must lie in a row set of size at most t (or the theorem is restricted to the first exposed seam); without |R_D|<=t the t*(p-1) cell count does not follow.

The row-sharp Q prefix-atom reductions (was PR #397): additive stratum bound |R_prim|<=|G_gen_support|+|D_full_rank_prim|+|E_ret| with |E_ret(z)|<=binom(16,7)=11440 imported-PROVED; the open primitive full-rank signed-defect certificate is the primal side of the holmbuar Fourier crux.

Mass-sensitive moment/orbit floor: any moment-only proof of R(z)<=2^Delta needs order r>=(w log2|B|-log2 L)/(Delta-log2 theta). Deployed r floors 94196/94991/641593/680397; M31-list binding row 680397 has no theorem-facing orbit gain (L=1) and even a full 2097152 orbit only reaches 680390; a 100000-moment theorem needs residual mass below 2^-17.835570.
nodes.evidence_quote: orbit amplification is not a finite solution mechanism

nodes.statement: M31-list c=2048 Chebyshev fixed-remainder quotient floor F_c=ceil(binom(1023,544)/p^32)=6796405 (exact lower floor, ~40.5% of budget); residual B*-F_2048=9980810 leaves 2.3237 bits above the identity average. Naive dyadic sum 16548620 (gap 228595) is NOT a counterpacket without a co-location theorem.

nodes.section: First unresolved defect size / surviving wall
nodes.statement: Follow-up on his own C1024 target: small-core (|core(S)|<=65) c=1024 defects collapse to the c=2048 factor-through floor; the first live non-factor-through wall is the m=67 even-defect divisor count (CAP25-V13-M31-T2048-EVEN-DEFECT-DIVISOR-COUNT), a degree-34 divisor count with a 32-even-completion layer choosing 511 of 956 antipodal pairs; NOT paid by cor:bc-one-pencil.
nodes.evidence_quote: first live non-factor-through wall is the m=67 even-defect divisor count

nodes.statement: Precision reconciliation of the finite-Q moment-order floor r0=ceil(w log2|B|/Delta_Q): confirms the maintainer's 94196/94991/641593/680397 exactly under the real-average convention and supersedes exactly two #384 entries (KB-list 94992->94991, M31-MCA 641584->641593).

Binding-row kappa calibration: measured kappa = R_prim <= 1.221 on every heavy row vs the binding M31-list budget kappa <= 8.4152; heaviness crossover avg* ~ 1205 sits 3-7 orders below deployed heaviness; the atom is SUPPORTED within coverage.

The p^{w/2} concentration floor: every second-moment (r=2 / Cauchy-Schwarz / Fourier-Plancherel) route for the KB-MCA target is DEAD by 1,045,396.58 bits, global and per-stratum; the named crux is the signed-e_m inverse theorem. target_floor=274836936291722953=K_rem*avg (K_rem=4805007), reserving t*p=143763024447376.

The signed-e_m L1 target (STAR) is exactly a Fourier participation-ratio bound PR(Rhat)<=nu*=(K_rem-1)^2/(Gamma2-1); nu*_ref=(K_rem-1)^2=23088082660036; the L-infinity per-direction route is separately dead by 2,090,815.35 bits; the binding M31-list target is effective support ~55.

The (STAR)<=>PR<=nu* equivalence transfers verbatim to the masked residual E_Q; support masking passes the M31-list budget at the #413 row (M_gen pulls the L1 triangle 10.472846->5.967<8.4152, tau=0.4107) but ONLY CONDITIONAL on the still-open lift-class support-removal cost model, and the win is a tau<1 raw-avg-units artifact.

The finite-field lift-class cost model is unpayable: keep-one-per-target lift-class removal at cost w*p is NOT a row-indexed payment (structural margin 2^2090779.6; removed classes/t*p=2^2090812.77, removed supports/B*=2^2090815.35). Resolves #416's condition negatively at deployment. K_raw=4807520, K_rem=4805007, retained=11440.
nodes.evidence_quote: keep-one-per-target is not a row-indexed payment

nodes.status: AUDIT / GREEN(descent D PROVED; L1..L4 OPEN)
nodes.statement: conj:Q divisor-lattice rung audit at KB-MCA a0+1=1116048: descent identity (D) PROVED; charge=35624 of K_raw=4807520, residual primitive budget 4771896 (0.0107-bit loss); wall reduced to the primitive core maxPi_0 plus a budget-negligible 4-rung ladder L_1..L_4 (OPEN-REDUCTION). VERDICT GREEN.

conj:Q rung audit at M31-MCA a0+1=1116024: same descent (D) PROVED, but the pessimistic same-bar 3-rung ladder EXCEEDS the tiny budget K_raw=9 by ~4.66x (charge=42, nominal residual -33); NOT a refutation and the edge does not move, but the row demands genuinely tighter row-specific quotient flatness than KB.

def:q-row-atom: R_Q^max = |B|^w max_z |P_Q(z)|/C(n,a_+) <= 2^{Delta_Q}; equivalently max_z|P_Q(z)|<=B*. This atom bound is the missing theorem the whole map is about. Deployed a+ 1116048 (KB-MCA) / 1116023 (M31-list).
prob:row-sharp-q: prove max_z|P_Q(z)|<=R_Q^row C(n,a_+)|B|^{-(a_+-K)} with the exact row overhead; a moment proof must satisfy log2 Gamma_r + (a_+-K)log2|B| <= r Delta_Q at the actual finite margin.

prop:q-exact-target: full-budget Q atom target per row; B*_KB=274980728111395087, B*_M31=16777215; budget ratios 4807520.9295 / 4226236.5253 / 9.5722 / 8.4152; the binding Q problem is the Mersenne-31 list row (~8.42x).

nodes.status: AUDIT(clarification; cross-lineage AGREEMENT, not a contradiction)
nodes.statement: target_floor=274836936291722953 (=K_rem*avg, the Q-actionable residual max-fiber target) is used identically by avdeevvadim (topseam) and holmbuar (#412/#414). It is a DISTINCT object from grande_finale B*=274980728111395087 (the full per-fiber budget); they differ by ~t*p=143763024447376. DO NOT CONFLATE target_floor with B*; the two Route-D lineages agree on target_floor to the digit.

The moment-order-floor entries KB-list 94992->94991 and M31-MCA 641584->641593 from #384 are SUPERSEDED by the reconciliation note (rounded 4-decimal margin input vs exact real-average Delta_Q); resolved in-tree, not an open contradiction.

M31-MCA is the unique convention-sensitive moment row: r_ceil=641594 vs r_real=641593 (the tiny margin amplifies the log2(ceil) vs log2(real) gap); the maintainer's table uses the real-average column.
KB rung-audit aggregate charge is a +/-1 convention: ceil 35624 (adopted, conservative) vs floor 35623 (Lane C's own script); every per-rung share and verdict is identical either way.
nodes.evidence_quote: aggregation-convention choice, not a new arithmetic error

speculative_edges.label: ANALYSIS-CONJECTURAL
speculative_edges.rationale: Hughes's |T|<=H2 free-1 high card and avdeev's charged-row R_D top-seam compiler both bound the KB-MCA Route-D residual toward A_SP<=t*p=e*p, but NO source identifies the free-1 high/|T| object with the charged-row object; the topseam explicitly states it 'does not supersede a full Route-D residual support certificate' (#423). The identification is plausible but unproven.

speculative_edges.label: ANALYSIS-CONJECTURAL
speculative_edges.rationale: Hughes's free-1 pairs (f_U-f_V constant) and avdeev's Rule-2 multiplicity-aware SP/Pade shift-pairs (U!=U0 same cell) are both shift-pair structures on the KB-MCA residual, but no source demonstrably identifies them; the overlap is conjectural.

speculative_edges.label: ANALYSIS-CONJECTURAL
speculative_edges.rationale: Danny's M31 m=67 even-defect divisor wall has a named 'M31-PLANTED-RESIDUAL-SHIFT-PAIR-INVERSE' incidence route, structurally analogous to avdeev's KB weighted_primitive_sp_pade_bound; but they are different rows (M31 vs KB) and no source links them.
